---
title: "4.2 Introduction to Using Data Sets"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Introduction to Using Data Sets

### A data set is a collection of specific pieces of information

This topic keeps the textbook introduction before Java data structures appear.

::: {.tight-list}
- a <span class="term">data set</span> stores related pieces of information
- the information can be organized in a chart or table
- the table helps students plan how an algorithm should process the values
:::

## Tables and Charts Represent Data

### Structured data is easier to inspect and process

| First Name | Last Name | Email | GPA |
|---|---|---|---:|
| Ana | Li | ana@example.com | 3.9 |
| Marco | Diaz | marco@example.com | 3.4 |
| Priya | Shah | priya@example.com | 3.7 |

Spreadsheet software stores data in this tabular form and provides tools to calculate with it.

## Plan the Algorithm from the Table

### Students process one value at a time

To find an average GPA, the algorithm needs to:

::: {.tight-list}
- visit each row
- access each GPA value
- add the values together
- divide by the number of rows
:::

This is the core transition from table reading to algorithm design.

## Loops and Selection in Dataset Reasoning

### Data processing often combines repetition and filtering

The textbook retains two main reasoning patterns:

::: {.tight-list}
- use <span class="term">loops</span> to process each value in sequence
- use <span class="term">selection</span> when only some rows meet a condition
:::

For example, an honor-roll algorithm would examine each GPA and print only those at `3.5` or higher.

## Bridge to Java Data Structures

### The same reasoning will soon move into arrays

This topic is intentionally lightweight because it prepares students for the next step:

::: {.tight-list}
- represent many values together
- access them one at a time
- combine loops with conditions to answer a question
:::

The next lessons transfer that same logic into Java arrays.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- add another row to a simple spreadsheet-style table
- order the pseudocode steps for average GPA
- write short pseudocode for an honor-roll filter
- explain how one value is accessed and processed at a time
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define a <span class="term">data set</span>
- explain why tables and charts help with algorithm planning
- describe how an algorithm processes one value at a time
- explain how loops support dataset analysis
- explain how a condition can filter rows in a data set
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
